trigger_evals.query: make a conventional commit for the staged files
open a pull request for my branch
show the git diff without committing anything

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Commits without conversational approval, uses a subject-only conventional commit message, and stages only README.md.
checks: Does NOT ask the user for approval before committing
Commit message has NO body paragraph
Only README.md is included in the commit

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Commits only the intentionally staged files and mentions unstaged files as an FYI without staging them.
checks: Only the 2 pre-staged files (src/auth.py and src/auth_test.py) are committed
Unstaged files (config.yaml, docs/setup.md, src/utils.py) are NOT staged or committed
Mentions the unstaged files as an FYI to the user
Commit message uses a conventional commit prefix

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Proposes multiple separate commits for unrelated changes and presents all groups before executing anything.
checks: Proposes at least 2 separate commits for the unrelated changes
Presents all proposed groups to the user BEFORE executing any commits
Each proposed commit has an appropriate conventional commit prefix
Does not bundle all changes into a single commit
